items.text: OFP obtained and reviewed. Dispatcher name confirmed on OFP. OFP signed by Commander (or electronically via EFB). Soft copy: signed off electronically.
items.detail: An Operational Flight Plan shall be prepared for each intended flight. OFP can only be generated by the duty dispatcher's own user ID, indicating his name. Flight crew should confirm the dispatcher name on the OFP before signing. A hard/soft copy of each OFP shall be kept on file at the IOCC office originating the OFP.

items.text: Fuel planning reviewed: taxi fuel, trip fuel, contingency fuel, alternate fuel, FRF, additional fuel, extra fuel. Final fuel decision made by Commander. Discretionary fuel assessed; if required, reasons to be recorded on OFP or EFB.
items.detail: Final decision on the fuel to be carried rests with the Commander. Discretionary fuel is at the sole discretion of the Commander (PIC) and is independent --- it cannot be encouraged or discouraged. Reasons for discretionary fuel shall be recorded by PIC on OFP or via EFB interface for statistical purposes.

items.text: Meteorological information reviewed for departure aerodrome, destination, destination alternate(s) and en-route alternates. Expected met conditions at destination at estimated time of arrival confirmed at or above planning minima. Met information from State or aerodrome authorities only.
items.detail: On IFR flights the Commander shall only commence the flight when information is available indicating that the expected meteorological conditions, at the time of arrival, at the destination and/or required alternate aerodromes are at or above the planning minima. To dispatch a flight, Dispatch shall only use meteorological information provided or published by State or aerodrome authorities.

items.text: Aerodrome selection policy confirmed: destination and alternate aerodromes adequate for aircraft type and operation. Alternate(s) weather at estimated time of use at or above Turkish Airlines planning minima.
items.detail: Only aerodromes that are adequate for the type(s) of aircraft and operation(s) concerned shall be used for Turkish Airlines flights. An aerodrome shall be considered adequate if, at the expected time of use, the aerodrome is available and equipped with necessary ancillary services. Alternate planning minima as per approved Turkish Airlines criteria.

items.text: Personal crew documents confirmed valid and on person: crew licence with appropriate ratings, valid medical certificate, passport with visas (international flights), vaccination certificates if applicable, up-to-date pilot logbook, Turkish Airlines ID card.
items.detail: Domestic flights: valid crew member licence, valid medical certificate, instrument rating (co-pilots with CPL), special operations certificate if applicable, up-to-date pilot logbook, THY ID card. International flights (in addition): valid passport with appropriate visas, certificates of vaccination and Crew Member Certificate as applicable.

items.text: Check NOTOC and passenger manifest for Special Categories of Passengers (SCPs): PRM, sick passengers, pregnant passengers, unaccompanied minors, deportees, inadmissible passengers, persons in custody. Commander shall be notified in advance when SCPs are to be carried.
items.detail: Any passenger who is not capable of conducting required procedures in an emergency requires an appropriate able-bodied travel companion. SCPs shall not be allocated seats permitting direct access to emergency exits or where their presence could impede crew, obstruct emergency equipment, or impede emergency evacuation. The Commander shall be notified in advance when SCPs are to be carried.

items.text: Mass and balance documentation reviewed: take-off mass, landing mass, zero fuel mass, CG positions all within limits. DOW/DOI data source confirmed: EFB Library, Electronic Flight Folder, Flight Documents Folder, or ACARS data (checked in that priority order).
items.detail: The mass and balance documentation shall contain: name of the person who prepared the document, dry operating mass and corresponding CG of the aircraft (DOW/DOI data checked from EFB Library, EFF, Flight Documents Folder, ACARS data in that order), mass of fuel at take-off, mass of trip fuel, load components, take-off/landing/zero fuel mass, CG positions, and limiting mass and CG values.

items.text: Daily Check validity confirmed per aircraft type: A319/A320/A321 = 3 calendar days (DY); B737-700/800/900ER/-8MAX/-9MAX = 48 hours; B777/B787/A350 = 72 hours. DY interval may be counted from 00:00 UTC of the next day. If validity will be exceeded before maintenance facility, notify MCC before flight.
items.detail: Commander is responsible for confirming the validity period of the Daily Check (conducted/signed by maintenance staff only). Daily check shall NEVER be exceeded, even during the flight. If aeroplane flight pattern is such that the permissible limit is exceeded prior to reaching a maintenance facility, Commander shall notify Turkish Airlines Maintenance Control Centre for clarification and rectification before flight.

items.text: Walk-around pre-flight inspection performed by flight crew member. Scope: condition of all critical surfaces, control surface/landing gear locks removed, pitot/static covers and engine blanks removed, external surfaces free of ice/snow/sand/dust/contamination, cargo compartment doors closed and secured.
items.detail: Walk-around shall be conducted by a flight crew member. Maintenance staff walk-around is not acceptable as a substitute for the pre-flight inspection. During exterior inspection at EU aerodromes: all access panel doors openable without tools must be opened and checked (Secure Airport procedure). After check, flight crew shall ensure relevant doors are closed. Flight crew shall use fluorescent jackets or raincoats for ramp duties.

items.text: AML reviewed for defect write-ups, MEL and CDL deferred items. If defects present: checked for requirements, limitations, performance penalties and corrective actions. MEL validity dates verified. Expired MEL items render aircraft non-airworthy.
items.detail: If any defect write-ups are present: check for requirements, limitations, performance penalties and ensure corrective actions have been taken (item rectified or deferred per MEL, CDL, approved documentation, or approved for further flights by Turkish DGCA or Engineering Department). Check validity dates of MEL items --- expired dates render the aeroplane non-airworthy. Commander is responsible for accepting only airworthy aeroplanes for flight.

items.text: Engine oil contents verified per manufacturer recommendations. At transit stations: no abnormal or excessive oil consumption noted. Servicing confirmed correct. Minimum values and consumption rates per FCOM for applicable aircraft type.
items.detail: Prior to initiating a flight, the Commander shall ensure that the engine oil contents have been serviced in accordance with the aeroplane manufacturer recommendations. Prior to initiating a flight from a transit station, the Commander shall ensure that engine oil contents do not indicate any abnormal/excessive oil consumption.

items.text: Before boarding aircraft: mental note made of work in progress around aeroplane. Liaison with maintenance personnel to establish if any servicing or rectification of systems is in progress or due to take place prior to departure.
items.detail: Prior to boarding an aeroplane, the flight crew shall make a mental note of work in progress around the aeroplane, and liaise with the maintenance personnel to find out if any servicing or rectification of systems is in progress or due to take place prior to departure.

items.text: Required documents verified on board: AFM/CDL, Certificate of Registration, Certificate of Airworthiness, AOC certified copy, Operations Specifications, AML, MEL, OFP, NOTAMs/AIS brief, current meteorological information, mass and balance documentation, passenger/cargo manifests if applicable, NOTOC if applicable, Aircraft Security Search Checklist FR.73.0001, Occurrence Report forms, Emergency Response Guidance for DG incidents.
items.detail: The items listed shall be carried on board and shall, within a reasonable time of being requested, be presented by the Commander to authorised persons. In case of loss or theft of certificates, the operation may continue until the flight reaches its destination or a place where replacement documents can be provided.

items.text: AML fuel section reviewed. If filled by others: Total at Departure verified against actual fuel on board less fuel used during elapsed time. Uplift calculation: arrival fuel + uplift fuel = total gauge readings after fuelling. Values must agree.
items.detail: Fuel records shall be retained in the AML and related sections filled by Commander. If fuel information column is filled by other personnel, Commander shall ensure quantity in Total at Departure agrees with total fuel on board less any fuel used during elapsed time. Commander shall perform uplift calculation to confirm accuracy.

items.text: Fuel calculations confirmed within accuracy limits. If outside limits or any doubt: defect written in AML regarding fuel display/calculation systems and relevant departments notified.
items.detail: If calculations are outside accuracy limits or any doubt exists, the Commander shall write down a defect regarding the fuel display/calculation systems and notify relevant departments. If uplift check is within limits, calculated values filled in appropriate sections of AML.

items.text: AML pre-flight inspection section signed by Commander. Signature certifies: (1) airworthiness confirmed; (2) CDL compliance; (3) required instruments/equipment available and operable except per MEL; (4) relevant OM parts available; (5) required documents on board; (6) at least planned fuel and oil on board; (7) deferred defects accepted within MEL provisions. Co-pilot or maintenance staff signatures not acceptable.
items.detail: By signing the pre-flight inspection of the AML, the Commander is certifying compliance with Turkish DGCA/EASA regulations with respect to all seven items. If previous AML pages not present on board: record INFO: PRE-FLIGHT INSPECTION PERFORMED PRIOR TO THE FIRST FLIGHT in the defects section.

items.text: Sterile flight deck objectives recalled by Commander. Non-essential communication to be ceased from engine start through taxi and take-off until passing 10,000 ft AAL.
items.detail: Prior to the flight, during the preparation phase, the Commander should recall the objectives and importance of the sterile flight deck. Due to sterile cockpit procedure, first call between flight deck and cabin crew should be initiated from flight deck via interphone after take-off. If flight crew does not call cabin crew within 20 minutes after selecting seat belt signs on-off, cabin crew shall call flight crew.

items.text: Where duty begins at home base after crew rest: Commander or Cabin Chief verifies each cabin crew member is proficient in and familiar with relevant emergency items and procedures.
items.detail: Whenever a tour of duty for the whole crew begins at home base (duty after crew rest) the Commander or the Cabin Chief will check that each cabin crew member is proficient in and familiar with the relevant emergency items and procedures. This is a Commander responsibility that may be delegated to the Cabin Chief.

items.text: Cabin briefing concluded by Commander (or by delegation, Cabin Chief): emergency exit allocations assigned to each cabin crew member, responsibilities defined.
items.detail: The Commander, or by delegation of the Commander, the Cabin Chief shall conclude the cabin briefing by allocating emergency exit positions and responsibilities. This briefing covers emergency evacuation procedures, responsibility areas for security and safety checks, and pre-boarding cabin checks.

items.text: Commander has overall responsibility for ensuring all crew is fit for duty, even if a report of sickness is not received. If any crew member does not feel well: condition declared to relevant authority before duty commences.
items.detail: The Commander has an overall responsibility for ensuring that all of the crew is fit for duty, even if a report of sickness is not received. When a crew member does not feel well, he shall declare his unfit condition to relevant person/authority. Any sick/ill crew member unable to perform assigned flight duty shall inform Turkish Airlines Personnel Directorate/Health Services Management.

items.text: Required crew members confirmed on board or due on board at least 15 minutes prior to scheduled departure time. If Commander determines required crew will not be on board 15 minutes prior to departure: boarding shall not be approved or initiated.
items.detail: Crew scheduling department is responsible for ensuring required crew members are ready for duty on board at least 15 minutes prior to the scheduled departure time. If the Commander feels that the required crew members will not be on board 15 minutes prior to departure, he shall not approve/initiate the boarding process.

items.text: FR.73.0001 Aircraft Security Search Checklist completed for flight deck and cabin prior to passenger embarkation. Security search must be successfully completed before boarding commences.
items.detail: Prior to each departure, aeroplane shall be subjected to FR.73.0001 Aircraft Security Search Checklist in order to ensure that no prohibited articles are present on board. Prior to passenger embarkation, flight deck and the cabin security search shall be successfully completed. This is a mandatory requirement before any boarding approval is given.

items.text: At transit stops: cabin crew performs security search of aeroplane. If no findings: passenger embarkation may recommence. If finding: unidentified item not displaced, appropriate security department notified, local authority security procedures applied.
items.detail: Cabin crew members shall perform a security search of the aeroplane at transit stops in accordance with Subchapter 10.1.10. If the security search reveals no findings, passenger embarkation may recommence. If the security search reveals a finding: unidentified item(s) in the cabin shall not be displaced; appropriate security department shall be notified; security procedures of the local authority shall be applicable.

phases.title: Fuelling
phases.sub: Ramp --- With Passengers On Board: Prerequisites Apply

prereqs.text: Commander present in aeroplane. This requirement does not prevent Commander from conducting exterior inspection.
prereqs.detail: Whenever passengers are embarking, on board or disembarking, the Commander shall be present in the aeroplane. Per 8.2.1.4(B)(1), Commander must be present and complete the pre-fuelling briefing before refuelling commences.

prereqs.text: Commander has briefed Cabin Chief AND ground operations representative: (1) designated exits for rapid deplaning/emergency evacuation identified; (2) routes to exits confirmed unobstructed; (3) ramp area beneath exits to be kept clear by ground crew; (4) expected duration of refuelling communicated.
prereqs.detail: Before starting refuelling/defuelling, Commander shall brief Cabin Chief and ground operations representative about: which cabin exits are designated for rapid deplaning or emergency evacuation, that routes to such exits are unobstructed, that the ramp area beneath is kept clear by the ground crew supervising the refuelling, and the expected duration of the operation.

prereqs.text: If required by local regulations: applicable authorities informed that refuelling/defuelling will take place with passengers embarking, on board or disembarking.
prereqs.detail: If required by local regulations, the Commander shall inform the applicable authorities that the refuelling or defuelling process will be taking place with passengers embarking, onboard or disembarking.

prereqs.text: Signs configured: Fasten Seat Belt signs --- OFF. No Smoking sign --- ON (if applicable). Emergency Lighting --- ARM.
prereqs.detail: CRITICAL: Seat belt signs are set OFF (not ON) during fuelling with passengers on board. No Smoking sign shall be ON. Emergency Lighting ARM. This is contrary to normal taxi/departure sign configuration and is a common error source.

prereqs.text: Two-way communication established and maintained via aeroplane intercommunication system with ground crew supervising refuelling throughout the entire operation.
prereqs.detail: Flight crew remaining in the flight deck shall establish and maintain two-way communication via the aeroplane intercommunication system with the ground crew supervising the refuelling or defuelling operation. This communication shall be maintained throughout the fuelling process.

prereqs.text: Cabin crew informed of beginning of refuelling/defuelling. Ready to inform cabin crew upon completion.
prereqs.detail: Flight crew shall inform the cabin crew of the beginning and completion of refuelling/defuelling when passengers are embarking, onboard or disembarking. Cabin crew members will make necessary announcements via PA to passengers before, during and upon completion of the procedure.

prereqs.text: Flight crew ready to initiate rapid deplaning if fuel spillage/leak. Ready to initiate emergency evacuation if fire or fire warning occurs.
prereqs.detail: Flight crew shall be ready to initiate: if necessary, a rapid deplaning if there is spillage or leak of fuel; if necessary, an emergency evacuation if there is a fire/fire warning. Cabin crew shall be ready to call flight crew via interphone if fuel vapor is detected or any hazard arises.

authItem.text: COMMANDER MUST AUTHORISE COMMENCEMENT OF REFUELLING. Refuelling is considered commenced when the fuel hose is connected to the aeroplane.

items.text: Upon advised of actual or estimated ZFW: fuel type and final fuel figure determined by Commander. Required final fuel figure in kg entered in Fuelling Order form provided by ground operations or fuelling company.
items.detail: Upon being advised of the actual or estimated ZFW, the Commander shall determine the fuel type and final fuel figure in accordance with procedures in this manual. The required final fuel figure, in kilograms, shall be entered in the Fuelling Order form.

items.text: Commander responsible for ensuring required fuel is on board and distributed appropriately prior to push-back or taxiing.
items.detail: The Commander is responsible for ensuring that the required fuel is on board the aeroplane and distributed appropriately prior to push-back or taxiing. This responsibility cannot be delegated.

items.text: After fuelling completed: uplift check performed using AML fuel records. Sum of (arrival fuel + uplift fuel) compared with total gauge readings after fuelling. Values must agree.
items.detail: In order to ensure fuel quantity system accuracy, after fuelling procedures have been completed, the Commander shall perform an uplift check using the fuel record figures in the AML. The uplift check consists of comparing the sum of the arrival fuel plus the uplift fuel with the total of the gauge readings after fuelling.

items.text: FMM (Fuel Order and Monitoring Message) form checked and signed by Commander at all international and domestic stations. Commander name, surname and ID number specified on form.
items.detail: In all international and domestic stations, Commander should check and sign the FMM form filled out by the ground staff as per Instruction TL.67.107. Commander name, surname and ID number must be specified on the form. If Turkish Airlines contracted ground operations/maintenance personnel exists assuring correct fuelling per local agreements without raising any doubt to Commander, there may be no need for Commander signature.

items.text: If technician not available after fuelling: Commander records uplifted fuel quantity and total fuel in kg in AML according to FFM form, or uplift calculation if FFM form not present.
items.detail: If a technician is not available after completion of fuelling procedures, the Commander shall record quantity of uplifted fuel and total amount of fuel in kilograms in the AML according to the FFM form or according to uplift calculation if FFM form is not present.

items.text: If total fuel uplifted exceeds amount requested: Commander notified immediately. Performance parameters re-evaluated to confirm no restrictions. If restrictions applicable: appropriate personnel notified for defuelling.
items.detail: If the total amount of fuel uplifted exceeds the amount of fuel requested by the Commander, the Commander shall be notified immediately. Commander shall re-evaluate performance parameters to ensure flight is not subject to performance restrictions. If performance restrictions applicable, Commander notifies appropriate personnel for defuelling.

items.text: Flights shall not depart with fuel less than required by official flight plan. Final decision rests with Commander.
items.detail: In all cases, Turkish Airlines flights shall not depart with fuel less than required by the official flight plan. Contingency fuel and extra fuel provided in the official flight plan may be consumed on the ground if provided by dispatch as precaution or with no specific reason; final decision rests with the Commander.

items.text: AML fuel section completed by Commander after refuelling. Fuel slip obtained if not received. Uplift calculation per 8.1.7.6(C) performed to determine correct fuel quantity on board.
items.detail: Commanders are responsible for entering the detailed information in the appropriate section of the AML after refuelling. Fuel slip regarding the uplift should be requested if it is not received and fuel information must be filled accordingly. Commander shall calculate fuel uplift according to Subchapter 8.1.7.6(C).

prereqs.text: Pre-flight inspection completed and AML signed by Commander confirming aircraft airworthy for intended flight.
prereqs.detail: Commander shall sign the Pre-flight Inspection section of the AML if convinced that the aeroplane is airworthy for the intended flight. This signature shall precede passenger embarkation.

prereqs.text: Mass and balance documentation finalised. LMC values within limits: narrow body max 500 kg, wide body max 1,000 kg, cargo max 2,000 kg.
prereqs.detail: Mass and balance documentation must be complete before boarding commences. Any LMC after completion of mass and balance documentation shall be brought to Commander attention and entered. If maximum allowed LMC exceeded: new mass and balance documentation required.

prereqs.text: FR.73.0001 Aircraft Security Search Checklist completed successfully. Flight deck and cabin security search accomplished.
prereqs.detail: Prior to each departure, aeroplane shall be subjected to FR.73.0001 Aircraft Security Search Checklist. Prior to passenger embarkation, flight deck and cabin security search shall be successfully completed. This is a pre-condition for boarding authorisation.

Whenever passengers are on board, the Commander shall be present in the aeroplane. Commander must be present before approving embarkation. This requirement does not prevent the Commander from conducting exterior inspection.

prereqs.text: Required crew members confirmed on board or will be on board at least 15 minutes prior to scheduled departure. If not: boarding shall not be approved.
prereqs.detail: If the Commander feels that the required crew members will not be on board the aeroplane 15 minutes prior to departure, he shall not approve/initiate the boarding process.

authItem.text: PASSENGER EMBARKATION SHALL NOT COMMENCE UNTIL APPROVAL ISSUED BY COMMANDER. At IST, SAW, ESB, ADB, AYT, COV, DLM, BJV automatic boarding start procedure applies per PR.10.60.004 where no irregularity exists.

items.text: From moment of embarking: Commander assumes responsibility for safety of all crew members, passengers and cargo (inclusive of baggage and mail) on board. Responsibility continues until Commander disembarks at end of flight.
items.detail: The Commander of the aeroplane is responsible for the safety of all crew members, passengers and cargo (inclusive of baggage and mail) on board an aeroplane, as soon as he embarks the aeroplane until he disembarks the aeroplane at the end of the flight.

items.text: Commander notified in advance of all Special Categories of Passengers (SCPs): PRM, sick passengers, pregnant passengers, unaccompanied minors, deportees, inadmissible passengers, persons in custody.
items.detail: The Commander shall be notified in advance when SCPs are to be carried on board. SCPs shall not be allocated seats permitting direct access to emergency exits or where their presence could impede crew members in their duties, obstruct access to emergency equipment, or impede emergency evacuation.

items.text: Passenger count performed when: parked at remote stand; directed by Line Information Form; Commander/cabin chief/ground staff suspect count discrepancy; no Line Information Form available for specific international station.
items.detail: For security reasons, the number of passengers who have passed through the boarding gate shall be the same as the number of passengers on board prior to closing the aeroplane doors for departure. If discrepancy found: recount, physical boarding pass check, and if unresolved, security search procedures apply.

items.text: Engine start shall not be initiated until all passengers/freight loaded, all aeroplane doors and hatches closed, and all ground equipment (except GPU if in use) removed from vicinity of aeroplane.
items.detail: Except for operations when dictated, engine start shall not be initiated until all passengers or freight have been loaded, the aeroplane doors and hatches have been closed, and all ground equipment, except for a ground power unit when used, have been removed from the vicinity of the aeroplane.

items.text: Prior to take-off: CABIN READY report received from Cabin Chief via interphone or cabin ready push button. If CABIN NOT READY received: take-off shall not commence until CABIN READY confirmation obtained.
items.detail: The Cabin Chief, having received the appropriate cabin report from each cabin crew member, shall report CABIN READY to the flight crew. If the cabin chief decides that the cabin is not ready, he shall immediately inform the Commander via interphone CABIN NOT READY. Take-off shall not be initiated if CABIN NOT READY has been received.

items.text: Prior to commencing push-back: slides armed by cabin crew or qualified personnel. All slides shall remain armed during taxi, take-off, landing and when safe and practicable to do so.
items.detail: Prior to commencing push-back, slides shall be armed by the cabin crew or qualified personnel. All slides shall remain armed during taxi, take-off, landing and when safe and practicable to do so. When the aeroplane comes to rest at the destination, slides shall be disarmed by qualified personnel.

items.text: In case of LMC on mass and balance documentation after door closure: changes may be relayed to Commander via radio or ground service interphone. Both flight crew and ground staff shall agree on acceptance.
items.detail: In case of LMC on mass and balance documentation after door closure, changes may be relayed to the Commander via radio or the ground service interphone. Flight crew and ground staff shall agree on acceptance of such changes.

items.text: Engine start not initiated until all aeroplane doors and hatches closed and all ground equipment (except GPU if in use) removed from vicinity of aeroplane.
items.detail: Except for operations when dictated, engine start shall not be initiated until all passengers or freight have been loaded, the aeroplane doors and hatches have been closed, and all ground equipment, except for a ground power unit when used, have been removed from the vicinity of the aeroplane.

items.text: Commander confirms all crew fit for duty prior to departure. If any crew member reports unfit: appropriate action taken before commencing flight.
items.detail: The Commander has an overall responsibility for ensuring that all of the crew is fit for duty, even if a report of sickness is not received. Any sick/ill crew member unable to perform assigned flight duty shall inform Turkish Airlines Personnel Directorate/Health Services Management about his condition.

items.text: Clean aircraft concept: shall not commence take-off if there is reason to believe that frost, ice or snow is adhering to the wings, control surfaces or other critical surfaces, except as explicitly permitted in the AFM.
items.detail: The clean aircraft concept is fundamental to safe operations. Any contamination on critical aerodynamic surfaces can adversely affect performance and controllability. No exception is permitted unless explicitly stated in the relevant Aircraft Flight Manual.

items.text: In consultation with Commander: treatment type, surfaces and anti-icing requirements established before treatment commences. If treatment interrupted: Commander informed immediately with (1) reason, (2) actions, (3) expected delay. Before resuming: Commander informed and agreement obtained. If holdover time exceeded after interruption: restart required.
items.detail: Any de-icing/anti-icing treatment should be continuous and as short as possible. If treatment is interrupted: Commander must be given reason for interruption, actions to be taken, and expected time of delay. Before continuing treatment, Commander must be informed. If holdover time has been exceeded: application must be restarted, previously treated surfaces must be first de-iced then anti-iced.

items.text: Pre-take-off check: Commander continually monitors weather after de-icing/anti-icing. Prior to take-off: holdover time assessed as still appropriate; untreated surfaces confirmed not contaminated. Check performed as close to take-off as possible, normally from flight deck.
items.detail: The commander shall continually monitor the weather conditions after the performed de-icing/anti-icing treatment. Prior to take-off he shall assess whether the applied holdover time is still appropriate and whether untreated surfaces may have become contaminated. Where a crew member observes frost, ice or snow adhering to the wings before commencing take-off, that crew member shall immediately report to the Commander.

items.text: De-icing/anti-icing details entered in AML outbound page: TYPE (1/2/3/4) / MIX RATIO (%) / START TIME (local HH:MM) / DATE (local DD/MM/YYYY) / APPLICATION STEP (ONE or TWO) / HOLDOVER TIME (minutes) / BRAND NAME.
items.detail: Commanders are responsible for entering the detailed information in AML (outbound page) when ground de-icing/anti-icing has been accomplished. ANTI-ICING CODE format: TYPE/brand name/mix ratio/start time local/date/application step/POST DE-ICING/ANTI-ICING CHECK COMPLETED. Anti-icing fluid rate is always written first. Holdover time is calculated by Commander per fluid type, mixture ratio and meteorological conditions.

items.text: Anti-icing code received from de-icing operator confirming: post de-icing/anti-icing check completed. Commander confirms holdover time acceptable for departure window given current meteorological conditions.
items.detail: Standard communication: De-icing operator confirms completion with post de-icing/anti-icing check completed. Commander assesses holdover time against current met conditions and confirms acceptable window for departure. Holdover time calculation is the Commander's responsibility.

items.text: Commander shall only commence flight when information available indicating expected met conditions at destination and required alternate(s) at or above planning minima at time of arrival. Met information from State or aerodrome authorities only.
items.detail: On IFR flights the Commander shall only commence the flight when information is available indicating that the expected meteorological conditions at the time of arrival at the destination and/or required alternate aerodromes are at or above the planning minima.

items.text: During take-off: each flight crew member required on duty in flight crew compartment shall be at assigned station. On revenue flights: no flight crew member vacates pilot seat below 20,000 ft AAL for purpose of transferring duties, except during base training flights.
items.detail: During take-off and landing each flight crew member required to be on duty in the flight crew compartment shall be at the assigned station. On revenue flights, flight crew members shall not vacate a pilot seat below 20,000 feet AAL for the purpose of transferring duties to another flight crew member except during base training flights.

items.text: During take-off: each crew member properly secured by all safety belts and restraint systems (harnesses) provided.
items.detail: During take-off and landing, and whenever deemed necessary by the Commander in the interest of safety, each crew member shall be properly secured by all safety belts and restraint systems (e.g. harnesses) provided.

items.text: Sterile flight deck in effect from push-back / engine start through taxi and take-off until passing 10,000 ft AAL. Non-essential communication ceased.
items.detail: Prior to the flight, during the preparation phase, the Commander should recall the objectives and importance of the sterile flight deck. Due to sterile cockpit procedure, first call between flight deck and cabin crew should be initiated from flight deck via interphone after take-off. If flight crew does not call cabin crew within 20 minutes after selecting seat belt signs on-off, cabin crew shall call flight crew.

items.text: If full take-off thrust used: note entered in AML defect section --- INFO: Full take-off thrust used for departure. For tracking purposes only, maintenance action not required.
items.detail: The Commander shall make a note in the defect section of the AML when full take-off thrust has been used. Example: INFO - Full take-off thrust used for departure. The purpose is for tracking purposes only and a maintenance action is not required.

items.text: Nothing in noise abatement procedures shall prevent Commander from exercising authority for the safe operation of the aeroplane. National requirements shall take precedence if different from Turkish Airlines procedures.
items.detail: Turkish Airlines uses two take-off procedures satisfying NADP 1 (close-in noise abatement) and NADP 2 (distant noise abatement). Nothing in these procedures shall prevent the pilot-in-command from exercising authority for the safe operation of the aeroplane.

items.text: Met conditions monitored throughout en-route: destination, destination alternate(s), en-route alternates. Continue toward planned destination only when latest information indicates conditions at or above applicable minima at ETA. Met information from State or aerodrome authorities only.
items.detail: During en route phase of flight, the Commander shall monitor meteorological conditions for: Destination aerodrome, Destination alternate aerodrome(s) if applicable, En route alternate aerodromes if applicable. Commander shall only continue toward planned destination when latest info indicates conditions at or above applicable minima.

items.text: MINIMUM FUEL: when (1) committed to land at specific aerodrome AND (2) any change to existing clearance may result in landing with less than planned final reserve fuel --- declare MINIMUM FUEL to ATC.
items.detail: The commander shall advise ATC of a MINIMUM FUEL state by declaring MINIMUM FUEL when committed to land at a specific aerodrome and any change to the existing clearance may result in landing with less than the planned final reserve fuel. Specific aerodrome may be the destination, alternate or any other safe landing option.

items.text: MAYDAY FUEL: when usable fuel calculated to be available upon landing at nearest aerodrome where safe landing can be made is less than planned final reserve fuel --- broadcast MAYDAY MAYDAY MAYDAY FUEL.
items.detail: The commander shall declare a situation of fuel emergency by broadcasting MAYDAY MAYDAY MAYDAY FUEL when the usable fuel calculated to be available upon landing at the nearest aerodrome where a safe landing can be made is less than the planned final reserve fuel.

items.text: Flight crew member remains at assigned station unless absence necessary for performance of duties or physiological needs. At least one suitably qualified pilot remains at controls at all times.
items.detail: During all other phases of flight each flight crew member required to be on duty in the flight crew compartment shall remain at the assigned station, unless absence is necessary for the performance of his duties in connection with the operation or for physiological needs, provided at least one suitably qualified pilot remains at the controls at all times.

items.text: Controlled rest (if applicable): Commander informs Cabin Chief of crew member's intention to take controlled rest and end of rest period. Every 20 minutes contact established between non-resting flight crew member and cabin crew.
items.detail: In the case of two-crew operations, to ensure that the non-resting flight crew member remains alert, Commander shall inform the Cabin Chief of the intention of the flight crew member to take controlled rest and the end of that rest. Every 20 minutes, contact shall be established between the non-resting flight crew member and the cabin crew, either via interphone or by a visit to the flight deck.

items.text: When ACAS/TCAS RA produced: (1) PF immediately conforms to RA even if conflicting with ATC unless it would jeopardise safety; (2) Notify ATC of RA requiring deviation; (3) When conflict resolved: return to acknowledged ATC clearance and notify ATC.
items.detail: When a RA indication is produced by ACAS/TCAS: The pilot flying shall immediately conform to the RA indications, even if this conflicts with an ATC instruction, unless doing so would jeopardize safety. The flight crew shall notify ATC of any RA requiring deviation as soon as workload permits. When conflict resolved: promptly return to acknowledged ATC clearance and notify ATC.

items.text: In-flight fuel checks carried out at intervals not exceeding 60 minutes. On flights less than 60 minutes: one fuel check before top of descent. Commander ensures manual entry of fuel quantity check on EFB Route Leg completed. Relevant fuel data recorded.
items.detail: The Commander shall ensure that fuel checks are carried out in-flight at intervals not exceeding 60 minutes. On a flight of less than 60 minutes: one fuel check before top of descent. The relevant fuel data shall be recorded. Many recordings are done automatically by Turkish Airlines. Commander shall ensure that manual entry of fuel quantity check on the EFB Route Leg is completed as per items required.

items.text: For any defect detected during flight requiring AML recording: ACARS free text description of the defect should be sent before 20,000 ft AAL. AML post-flight defect recording page must be selected YES for any post-flight defect recording.
items.detail: For the defects detected during flight which require an AML recording, an ACARS free text description of the defect should be sent before 20,000 ft AAL. The AML post-flight defect recording page must be selected as YES for any post-flight defect recording.

items.text: Latest met information confirms destination at or above applicable minima at ETA. If conditions below minima: continuation requires confirmation that suitable alternate available at or above minima.
items.detail: Commander shall only continue toward planned destination when latest info indicates conditions will be at or above applicable minima at time of arrival. If destination is below minima, continuation requires confirmation that a suitable alternate is available with conditions at or above minima.

items.text: Stabilised approach: approach flown in controlled and appropriate manner in terms of configuration, energy and flight path from 1,000 ft AAL down to TCH. If not stabilised at 1,000 ft AAL: go-around executed.
items.detail: Stabilized approach (SAp) is defined as an approach flown in a controlled and appropriate manner in terms of configuration, energy and control of the flight path from 1000 ft AAL down to TCH. A stabilised approach addresses a set of measurable parameters to maintain safe conclusion of any flight.

items.text: At approximately 5,000 ft AAL: PM makes landing announcement --- CABIN CREW BE SEATED FOR LANDING. Cabin crew occupies designated stations and fastens seat belts/harnesses.
items.detail: Landing announcement for cabin crew shall be made when passing about 5,000 feet AAL by the PM as CABIN CREW BE SEATED FOR LANDING. Cabin crew members shall at the latest, on receipt of instruction from the flight deck, occupy their designated stations and fasten their seat belts/harnesses.

items.text: CABIN READY report received from Cabin Chief before landing initiated. If CABIN NOT READY received: landing shall not be initiated until CABIN READY confirmation obtained.
items.detail: The Cabin Chief, having received the appropriate cabin report from each cabin crew member shall report CABIN READY to the flight crew by interphone or cabin ready push button. If CABIN NOT READY received, landing shall not be initiated until CABIN READY is confirmed.

items.text: During landing: each crew member properly secured by all safety belts and restraint systems provided.
items.detail: During take-off and landing, and whenever deemed necessary by the Commander in the interest of safety, each crew member shall be properly secured by all safety belts and restraint systems provided.

items.text: During landing: each flight crew member required on duty in flight crew compartment shall be at assigned station.
items.detail: During take-off and landing each flight crew member required to be on duty in the flight crew compartment shall be at the assigned station.

items.text: Cabin doors shall not be opened before anti-collision beacon switched OFF at gate/parking position. Cabin Chief confirms all slides disarmed. Commander's permission required before any cabin door is opened.
items.detail: The cabin or compartment doors shall not be opened before the anti-collision beacon has been switched OFF upon arrival at the gate/parking position. Cabin Chief shall confirm with the Commander that all slides are disarmed, thereafter with Commander's approval may open the applicable cabin doors (upon receiving final approval from ground personnel).

items.text: Passenger disembarkation commences only after receiving initial approval from Commander and final approval from appropriate ground personnel. Crew members remain at stations until all passengers have disembarked.
items.detail: Passenger disembarkation shall only commence after receiving the initial approval from the Commander and the final approval from the appropriate ground personnel. Crew members shall remain on board (at their stations) until all of the passengers have disembarked the aeroplane.

items.text: All known or suspected technical defects recorded in AML in English with full details. Flight safety related cabin defects reported by Cabin Chief and recorded by Commander. Each defect with sequence item number. Defects in capital letters. Maximum two defects per AML page.
items.detail: Commanders are responsible for entering any known defects (in English including full details) affecting airworthiness or safe operation. Flight safety related cabin defects must be reported to Commander by Cabin Chief. If defect written by Commander, Commander checks PILOT box, signs SIGN section and writes company ID number on ID NO section.

items.text: If no defects noted upon completion of Parking/Shutdown Checklist: enter NIL INBOUND in first section of AML. Remaining empty defect sections shall NOT be crossed out.
items.detail: If no defects are noted for the post-flight (upon completion of the Parking/Shutdown Checklist), the Commander of the inbound flight shall enter NIL INBOUND statement in the first section of the AML and shall not cross out any remaining empty defect sections --- these are to be filled by maintenance staff or the outbound flight crew.

items.text: If conditional inspection event occurred: recorded in AML and maintenance notified. Events include: hard landing, overweight landing, severe turbulence, overspeed (flap/slat/MMO/VMO/gear/tires), high energy stop, lightning strike, extreme dust, tail strike, bird strike, swarm of insects.
items.detail: During ground or flight operations, events may occur which require maintenance inspection after flight (Conditional Inspections). An event that requires maintenance inspection shall be recorded in the AML. Contact maintenance department if any doubt exists about whether a maintenance inspection is required.

items.text: If full take-off thrust was used this flight: confirm INFO note present in AML defect section --- INFO: Full take-off thrust used for departure.
items.detail: The Commander shall make a note in the defect section of the AML when full take-off thrust has been used. The purpose is for tracking purposes only and a maintenance action is not required.

items.text: All flight crew including Commander shall submit ASR (Air Safety Report) to Turkish Airlines within 24 hours for any incident that endangered or could endanger safety of operation. Turkish Airlines forwards to Authority within 72 hours. Failure to submit within 48 hours may result in administrative penalties.
items.detail: All flight crew on duty including Commander shall submit an ASR to Turkish Airlines of any incident that endangers or could endanger the safety of operation within 24 hours and Turkish Airlines shall forward the report to the Authority within 72 hours unless exceptional circumstances prevent this. If a report is not submitted within 48 hours, administrative penalties may be enforced by SVP Flight Operations/Chief Pilot.

items.text: After any incident or accident, Commander ensures: (a) passengers and crew safe; (b) aircraft secured; (c) flight recorders deactivated and recordings preserved if necessary; (d) evidence not lost; (e) Turkish and/or local government authority informed if necessary.
items.detail: The Commander shall ensure that: passengers and crew are safe, the aircraft is secured, flight recorders are deactivated and recordings are preserved (if necessary), evidence regarding the incident is not lost, and when necessary, Turkish government authority (if applicable) or local government authority is informed and help is requested.
